outlook.headline: Tariff headwinds are easing, though pockets of margin pressure remain
outlook.summary: The read-through is improving: mention frequency has collapsed and most commentary frames tariffs as manageable, easing, or neutralized rather than worsening. KLA, FormFactor, Analog Devices and DuPont suggest the trade-policy drag is fading over the next 1-2 quarters, while MKS and Amphenol show pass-through can keep the impact contained. The main laggards are Advanced Energy, TE Connectivity, Vishay and CTS, where costs and timing effects are still pressuring margins.

drivers.label: Tariff pressure easing or rates coming down
drivers.detail: Several companies said the issue is less severe now, with lower tariff rates and possible refunds improving the setup.

drivers.label: Offsetting mechanisms are working
drivers.detail: Pass-through, volume shifting, and cost neutralization are limiting the net damage from tariffs.

drivers.label: Lingering but not worsening dislocations
drivers.detail: Order and timing disruptions are still present, but the tone is more contained than escalating.

drivers.label: Residual margin headwinds in select names
drivers.detail: Some companies still called out ongoing tariff expenses, Section 232 changes, and inflation as active drags.

drivers.label: Policy fluidity still creates uncertainty
drivers.detail: The macro backdrop has not fully normalized, so abrupt policy shifts remain a risk.

risks: Tariff policy becomes more fluid or escalates again, reversing the easing trend.
Expected refunds or rate reductions fail to materialize, leaving the headwind in place.
Input-cost inflation and Section 232-related changes keep margins under pressure at the more exposed names.
